summary: explain's satisfies rationale narration (satisfies_clauses) still ignores lock-declared fills — fe2b22c fixed gate's pass/fail but explicitly scoped this half out

edit.description: resolve_kind_units (~L851-892, the shared per-kind Unit resolver both `gate` and custom-kind `explain` narration range over) grafts unit.satisfies_clauses (explain's rationale narration, consumed at ~L441 building read::CustomMember.satisfies) solely from surface_overlay — the pre-0016 own-path `+++`-document read whose only writers are #[cfg(test)]-only (confirmed 2026-07-08: zero production writers anywhere in src/). fe2b22c (2026-07-08) unioned declarations.satisfies into unit.satisfies for gate's pass/fail but its own commit message named satisfies_clauses out of scope ('a separate, narrower concern this entry doesn't touch'), so explain can silently disagree with gate: a custom member filled only via a lock-declared satisfies row (no surface_overlay document) reports as unfilled/no-rationale in explain while gate reports it filled — the exact narration/verdict split specs/model/contract.md's edge section forbids ('narration can never disagree with the verdict'). Union declarations.satisfies into unit.satisfies_clauses too, as document::Satisfies{requirement, rationale: None} entries (skip requirement names surface_overlay already supplied with real rationale), mirroring the existing unit.satisfies union block immediately below in the same function. Update the fn's doc comment (currently states satisfies_clauses 'stays surface_overlay-only') to match.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind member filled only via a lock-declared `declarations.satisfies` row (no surface_overlay-authored satisfies_clauses) narrates as filled through `explain`/`why` — no fabricated rationale, but never silently absent — matching gate's pass, mirroring the sibling `a_lock_declared_satisfies_row_fills_a_requirement_with_no_surface_overlay_authored` test fe2b22c added for gate

acceptance: explain's satisfies rationale narration for a custom-kind member never disagrees with gate's fill verdict, including for a member filled only via the lock's declarations.satisfies (no surface-authored rationale).
notes: scoped at b4649ff; blockedBy KIND-NAME-COLLISION-ADMISSIBILITY (job 5, 6d00c14 tick) — both edit main.rs::explain's same loop (L427-445): its builtin_defs skip at L428 vs this entry's satisfies_clauses read at L441, real overlap not just same-file; serialized per the disjoint-queue rule. Closes fe2b22c's deferred gap; scope is custom kinds only (resolve_kind_units + explain's custom_members loop). Also blocks RETIRE-DEAD-OWN-PATH-SURFACE-OVERLAY.

summary: The pre-0016 own-path '+++' surface-document mechanism (Workspace, Member::from_surface/to_document, surface_overlay, published_requirements) has zero production consumers — retire it

edit.description: Remove `surface_overlay` (~L822-833, now unused once the sibling entry lands the lock-sourced satisfies_clauses union) and its call in resolve_kind_units; drop `unit.published_requirements` population from it. Remove `explain`'s `let ws = Workspace::load(&workspace)?;` (~L451) and the `&ws` arg it threads into `read::explain` (~L488) — confirmed dead: `.temper`'s real layout (module-adjacent prose files like `.temper/rules/rust.md`) never matches the `+++`/per-id-subdirectory shape `Workspace::load` scans for, so `ws` is always empty for any real harness. In `union_published_requirements` (~L1027-1049), drop the per-member `features.published_requirements` branch and derive the roster from `assembly_requirements` (built from `declarations.requirements`, the lock) alone — confirmed complete: the SDK already unions `harness.require` and every `member.requires` into `declarations.requirements` at emit time (`sdk/src/declarations.ts` `requirementRows`, ~L269-296), so the member-published branch can only ever fire off the dead surface_overlay read; it never contributes for a real SDK-composed harness.

Remove the `Workspace` struct, `load`/`load_kinds`, and their accessor methods (~L34-95) — confirmed unused outside main.rs's two now-removed call sites and bundle.rs's (below). Remove the #[cfg(test)]-only `write_surface_skill`/`write_surface_rule` helpers (~L360-490) and the tests that exist solely to exercise `Workspace::load` through them; where a removed test's real assertion (e.g. a clause firing over a loaded member) is still meaningful, retarget it to construct the member via `frontmatter::Member`/`kind::Unit` directly rather than deleting the coverage.

Remove `Unit::from_surface_dir`/`from_member_document` (~L609-712) and the `published_requirements` field off `Unit` (~L605, now unpopulated everywhere). Remove or retarget their dependent tests (~L1080-1280) — several (`from_surface_dir_feeds_the_composed_extractor`, `..._lifts_clause_fields_into_frontmatter`, etc.) test Extraction/Features folding, which is still live; retarget those to build a `Unit` directly (a struct literal or `from_source_rooted`) rather than round-tripping through the retired surface writer.

Remove `Member::from_surface` (~L253-316) and `Member::to_document` (~L340+) plus their round-trip tests (~L640-670) — confirmed no caller outside the retired sites in main.rs/check.rs/kind.rs/builtin_kind.rs. Drop the `published_requirements` field off `Member` if `from_source_rooted` never populates it (verify before removing — it may already default-construct to empty there).

Remove `requirements()` (~L447) and `satisfies()` (~L416) — confirmed their only callers are `Member::from_surface` (frontmatter.rs:309) and `Unit::from_member_document` (kind.rs:676), both retired by this entry. Keep `Satisfies`/`PublishedRequirement` type definitions if `compose.rs`/`read.rs` still reference the types (only the parsing functions are dead, not necessarily the shapes).

Remove the now-dead `Workspace::load(surface)?;` validation call (~L168) — it was already a no-op fail-loud check (never errors for the real `.temper` layout, since `Workspace::load` cannot detect this format as malformed either). Update the module doc comment (~L27-29) which currently cites `Workspace::load` as the read mechanism. Do not invent a replacement validation — if `bundle` still wants a real malformed-surface check, that is a distinct product gap to surface, not to silently fill here.

Remove the #[cfg(test)]-only `surface_unit` helper (~L485-496, builds a Unit by round-tripping through `Member::to_document`/`Unit::from_member_document`, both retired) and retarget its dependent tests to construct a `Unit` directly instead of dropping their coverage of `Features` folding.

`read::explain`'s `workspace: &Workspace` parameter (~L230) and the `members()` helper's `workspace.skills()`/`workspace.rules()` half (~L119-128) become dead once main.rs stops constructing a `Workspace`; drop the parameter (callers thread `custom` alone) and simplify `members()` accordingly. Every downstream consumer already unions this listing with `by_kind` defensively (documented at ~L342-345, ~L1442-1447 — 'Workspace/custom listing lags by_kind'), so dropping the always-empty Workspace half changes no observable behavior for skill/rule members, which never had a live rationale source in production.

tests.asserts: explain's target resolution and narration for skill/rule/custom members is unchanged after `read::explain` drops its `Workspace` parameter — the unit-level test harness at tests/read_verbs.rs (which already constructs `Workspace::load` over an always-empty tmpdir, ~L75-84) is updated to match the simplified signature, not weakened

acceptance: No production or test code references check::Workspace, Member::from_surface/to_document, surface_overlay, document::requirements/satisfies, Unit::from_surface_dir/from_member_document, or Unit::published_requirements; cargo build/test/clippy stay green with no loss of Features/Extraction test coverage.
notes: scoped at b4649ff. Confirmed 2026-07-08: zero references to Workspace/from_surface/to_document/surface_overlay in install.rs, drift.rs, engine.rs, roster.rs, compose.rs — the mechanism is fully isolated to the files listed. This is the pre-0016 own-path generation pipeline.md's current model (SDK/emit/lock) has no vocabulary for; retiring it is mechanical, not a design fork.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at 7cdf5ac (this tick): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
